notes: Base consolidada de regras aprovadas do PRzator. Cada regra ja reflete os ajustes definidos em 04-rule-approval-review.md do respectivo repo. Regras descartadas na revisao NAO estao neste arquivo.

rules.title: Controller Actions Should Stay Thin
rules.description: A controller action adds validation, calculations, status transitions, persistence orchestration, or integration behavior that would be safer in a model, component, lib, or event listener.

rules.title: Public Auth Exceptions Require Explicit Review
rules.description: A controller broadens public access by adding or changing Auth->allow(), a login bypass, or a publicly reachable admin route without narrow justification and regression coverage.

rules.title: Preserve Shared Document Normalization and Alphanumeric CNPJ Handling
rules.description: A PHP-side change reintroduces numeric-only CNPJ assumptions, bypasses shared normalization helpers, or duplicates document-cleaning logic instead of reusing the existing FuncoesLib and model validation path.

rules.title: Preserve Event-Based Side Effects in Core Workflows
rules.description: A change to user or invoice lifecycle code removes existing CakeEvent dispatch, bypasses registered listeners, or inlines external side effects directly into core writes without a compelling reason.

rules.title: Review Paginated and VirtualField Queries for Cost
rules.description: A change adds virtual fields, correlated subqueries, wide joins, recursive loading, or broader contain/paginate settings to admin listings without considering query cost and data volume.

rules.title: High-Risk Behavior Changes Need Cake Test Coverage
rules.description: A change to model callbacks, auth rules, billing flows, shared libs, or event/listener behavior should add or update fixture-backed tests under php/app/Test/Case when the affected behavior is not already covered.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Original vinha 'high' e sem escopo; reduzido para medium e restrito a callbacks de model, auth, billing, libs compartilhadas, listeners/integracoes.

rules.title: Secrets and Environment Endpoints Must Not Be Hardcoded in App Logic
rules.description: A change hardcodes credentials, tokens, private keys, queue endpoints, or environment-specific service URLs in controllers, models, libs, or views instead of reading them from the config layer and environment-backed settings.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Regra original misturava segredo hardcoded com decisao arquitetural de onde config deveria morar, e tinha fileGlobs amplo demais (php/app/**/*.php). Separado para focar so em segredo/token/credencial/endpoint, com escopo mais claro.

rules.title: CakePHP 2 File/Class Naming Mismatch
rules.description: In CakePHP 2, the filename must exactly match the class name including case. A mismatch causes silent load failures. Bad: file `emailSender.php` containing `class EmailSender`. Good: file `EmailSender.php` containing `class EmailSender`.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Movida da baseline default para repo-specific porque esta cobranca so faz sentido nos repos legados em CakePHP 2.

rules.title: CakePHP 2 Undeclared $uses or $components
rules.description: A Model or Component is used inside a CakePHP 2 controller without being declared in $uses or $components. This causes a fatal error at runtime. Bad: calling `$this->Payment->save()` without `public $uses = ["Payment"]`. Good: declare every Model in `$uses` and every Component in `$components` before using them.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Movida da baseline default para repo-specific porque esta cobranca so faz sentido nos repos legados em CakePHP 2.

rules.title: planos.valor Must Store Monthly Equivalent Only
rules.description: In this subscriptions stack, `planos.valor` is treated as the base monthly value, while billing-cycle totals and `valor_destaque` are derived separately for checkout and Iugu flows. A change that persists a quarterly, semi-annual, or annual total directly to `planos.valor`, or mixes `valor` and `valor_destaque` semantics when saving plans, will corrupt pricing and downstream calculations.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Movida da baseline default para repo-specific e estreitada para os pontos que realmente escrevem ou sincronizam o pricing de planos neste legado.

rules.title: Controllers Stay Orchestration-Focused
rules.description: A controller action introduced domain decisions, persistence rules, or multi-step workflow logic that should live in a Table or Service class instead of staying in the request/auth/response layer.

rules.title: New V1 Endpoints Need Route Permission and Tests
rules.description: A new or changed `/v1` endpoint was shipped without the matching explicit route entry, `api_tipo_permissoes` coverage, and controller integration tests for the allowed actors and HTTP methods.

rules.title: API Errors Flow Through AppException Or ApiRest
rules.description: New API code returned ad hoc error payloads, swallowed exceptions, or leaked raw exceptions instead of throwing `AppException` or framework HTTP exceptions and translating them through `ApiRestComponent::tratarException()`.

rules.title: Cache Sensitive Endpoint Changes Must Prove Fresh Behavior
rules.description: A change to routes, API key permissions, or cached lookup tables landed without explicit cache isolation in tests or an operational note about `_cake_routes_` and `long` cache invalidation.

rules.title: Controller Endpoint Tests Should Use Real Headers Fixtures And Cache Setup
rules.description: Endpoint tests were added as narrow unit tests only, or skipped the auth headers, fixtures, and cache setup that this repo depends on for realistic controller behavior.

rules.title: Persisted User Text Needs Validation And Sanitization At The Boundary
rules.description: Persisted free-text fields, or fields that can later be rendered by a client, email, or webview, bypassed table-level validation or sanitization, or introduced new HTML/URL handling without an explicit safe encoding strategy.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Original falava em qualquer conteudo 'stored or returned to clients', amplo demais para API. Restrito a texto livre persistido, campos renderizados depois (cliente/email/webview) e bypass de validacao/sanitizacao de Table.

rules.title: Browser Callable Write Endpoints Require Explicit CSRF CORS Review
rules.description: A state-changing endpoint reachable from browser clients was added or changed without documenting the intended authentication mode, origin policy, and why the current no-CSRF middleware posture remains safe. Should only trigger when there is concrete evidence the endpoint is reachable from a browser/web frontend, cookie/session, or a cross-origin sensitive flow -- not for every POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE by default.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Mantido medium; gatilho restrito a evidencia real de alcance por browser em vez de qualquer verbo de escrita.

rules.title: Avoid Interpolated Raw SQL in Models
rules.description: A model change introduced or modified Model::query() SQL that interpolates dynamic values directly into the statement instead of using model methods, updateAll(), or properly escaped parameters.

rules.title: Treat Cache Keys as Part of the Write Contract
rules.description: A change added or altered cached reads without using strict cache-miss checks or without invalidating the affected keys when the underlying records change.

rules.title: Preserve Route Order and Use Routing Arrays
rules.description: A routes.php change added or moved routes without considering top-to-bottom matching, redirects, or the existing split between API endpoints, current web UI, and legacy redirects.

rules.title: Preserve Domain Event Dispatch and Listener Coverage
rules.description: A change to demand, proposal, or account lifecycle behavior bypassed an existing Cake event, changed a payload shape consumed by a listener without aligning the other side, or altered a webhook/queue side effect without updating the corresponding listener.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Cobranca de teste tirada do centro da regra (virou consequencia, nao o foco). Foco mantido em contrato de evento/listener, nao em cobertura.

rules.title: Require CakePHP Test Coverage for Behavior Changes
rules.description: A behavior change in AppController, an auth/global boundary, an API controller, an important model lifecycle path, a listener/webhook/queue flow, or a write path with manual cache handling shipped without updating or adding CakePHP tests and fixtures/fakes (e.g. MessageQueue fake) needed to exercise it.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Reduzido de high generico para medium e restrito as areas de maior risco (AppController, auth/global boundary, API controllers, model lifecycle importante, listeners/webhook/fila, write paths com cache manual), para nao repetir a mesma cobranca ja aprovada em nimda/jobs-site/jobs-professional-api.

rules.title: CakePHP 2 File/Class Naming Mismatch
rules.description: In CakePHP 2, the filename must exactly match the class name including case. A mismatch causes silent load failures. Bad: file `emailSender.php` containing `class EmailSender`. Good: file `EmailSender.php` containing `class EmailSender`.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Movida da baseline default para repo-specific porque esta cobranca so faz sentido nos repos legados em CakePHP 2.

rules.title: CakePHP 2 Undeclared $uses or $components
rules.description: A Model or Component is used inside a CakePHP 2 controller without being declared in $uses or $components. This causes a fatal error at runtime. Bad: calling `$this->Payment->save()` without `public $uses = ["Payment"]`. Good: declare every Model in `$uses` and every Component in `$components` before using them.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Movida da baseline default para repo-specific porque esta cobranca so faz sentido nos repos legados em CakePHP 2.

rules.title: Do Not Implement Schema Changes by Editing Snapshot SQL
rules.description: A schema or domain-data change was introduced by hand in `database-image/sql/**` instead of shipping a Phinx migration under `php/db/migrations`, except for generated snapshot output produced by the repo workflow.

rules.title: Large-Table Index Migrations Must Be Operationally Defensive
rules.description: A migration that adds or drops indexes on hot or large tables omits existence checks, explicit `up()` and `down()` methods, or long-running timeout/session handling before issuing raw `ALTER TABLE` statements.

rules.title: Workflow Changes Must Preserve Least-Privilege Credentials and Permissions
rules.description: A GitHub Actions workflow change hardcodes credentials, removes secret or OIDC usage, or broadens `permissions` beyond what the job actually needs for checkout, AWS auth, and deployment work.

rules.title: Irreversible Or Data-Changing Migrations Need An Explicit Rollback Strategy
rules.description: A migration containing DML, raw SQL, or an operation that is not naturally reversible does not document an explicit safety strategy for reversal (accepted patterns: up()/down(), change() with a clear guard, or an explicitly documented no-op down() when reverting would destroy data).
rules.adjustmentNotes: Titulo/regra original exigia up()/down() como obrigacao absoluta, mas o repo tem migrations recentes legitimas usando change() com save()/isMigratingUp(). Ajustado para exigir estrategia explicita de seguranca, nao um formato fixo. Separado da regra de seeding para nao duplicar.

rules.title: New Tables Must Declare a Primary Key and Supporting Relationship Indexes
rules.description: A new table was added without a primary key (default from the framework or custom) or without a foreign key having reasonable index support (dedicated or composite) matching the repo's real access pattern.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Trocado 'primary key explicita' por 'primary key presente' (o repo usa tanto PK default do Phinx quanto customizada). Nao exige mais indice dedicado por relacao quando indice composto ja cobre.

rules.title: Shared Permission Or Lookup Data Seeding Must Be Idempotent and Reversible
rules.description: A migration seeding rows into shared permission, lookup, or domain tables lacks idempotency (e.g. `WHERE NOT EXISTS` or equivalent prior check) or a deterministic cleanup path in `down()` for environments where the migration may be reapplied with drift.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Restrito a tabelas compartilhadas de permissao/lookup/dominio (nao qualquer insert). Deve complementar, nao duplicar, a regra de jobs-professional-api sobre endpoint/rota/teste -- aqui o foco e a migration da permissao, la e o wiring/autorizacao do endpoint.

rules.title: planos Table Requires Raw SQL for ALTER
rules.description: The `planos` table contains legacy zero-date values that can make Phinx schema-builder alterations fail silently or error. Changes that alter `planos` should preserve the established raw-SQL pattern (for example the existing relaxed SQL-mode wrapper) instead of switching to `changeColumn()`, `addColumn()`, or similar builder calls on that table.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Movida da baseline default para jobs-database, onde a evidencia real ja aparece nas migrations que usam raw SQL e SQL mode relaxado para `planos`.

rules.title: Protect V1 Routes with Shared Middleware and Direct-Access Guards
rules.description: A new business endpoint under `v1` bypassed the shared auth/observability middleware or exposed a privileged system-only flow without preserving the current `acessoDireto` checks and caller-facing 401/404 behavior.

rules.title: Keep SQL-Backed Changes Compatible with the SQLite Test Bootstrap
rules.description: A schema-dependent or Eloquent-backed change broke the `banco-para-sqlite:executar` mirror step, fixture seeding, or the SQLite assumptions used by PHPUnit and CI.

rules.title: Preserve Webhook Envelope Shape and Non-Throwing Publish Semantics
rules.description: A webhook or queue publication changed its JSON envelope, queue selection, account routing, conditional publish gate, or failure behavior without explicit tests and logging expectations.

rules.title: Validate, Sanitize, and Whitelist Message Payloads Before Persistence
rules.description: Incoming message-related request fields were saved or forwarded without using the existing validation rules, HTML sanitization, allowed-field whitelist, file checks, or direct-access restrictions already encoded in the message write boundary.
rules.adjustmentNotes: fileGlobs original amplo demais (php/app/Services/**, php/app/Models/**, php/app/Http/Controllers/**, php/app/helpers.php), podia comentar em servicos/models/controllers fora do write path de mensagem. Restrito ao boundary real de persistencia de mensagem.

rules.title: Mock External Adapters in Tests and Assert Side-Effect Contracts
rules.description: A change to an adapter or side-effecting service (Mongo adapters, background writers, notifications, S3, RabbitMQ) shipped without focused tests that replace those collaborators with mocks and verify the expected contract. When the diff specifically touches WebhookPublisher, the webhook-envelope rule (jobs-chat-03) should be the primary rule, not this one.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Restrito a mudancas que cruzam adaptadores externos ou efeitos assincronos, evitando cobranca automatica para qualquer alteracao de service/model e sobreposicao com a regra de webhook.

rules.title: Single Transaction Per Multi-Model Write
rules.description: A service change that mutates multiple SQL-backed models should open one transaction and pass the shared connection into each participating model with `set_connection`, rather than letting each model write independently.

rules.title: Sanitize Prospecting Intake Before Prompting Or Persisting
rules.description: Public intake text or LLM prompt material must be sanitized with `sanitize_intake_text` or a reviewed equivalent before validation, storage, summarization, or prompt assembly.

rules.title: Router-Layer Requests Must Use validate_data and the Shared Response Wrapper
rules.description: A change in `app/http/routers/` parses JSON manually, validates ad hoc, or shapes the response outside the shared pattern instead of using `@validate_data`, contracts in `app/models/validation`, and the shared wrapper in `app/http/response.py`.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Titulo/framing original ('Thin Routers') puxava para a familia generica de 'controller fino' ja repetida em varios repos. Estreitado para o boundary local especifico (validate_data + Pydantic + Response), o que deixa de competir com jobs-professional-api/jobs-chat/jobs-manager.

rules.title: Preserve Mongo Change Logs For Mutable SQL Models
rules.description: A new Postgres model, a change to a model's base class/inheritance, or a new mutable business entity should continue to inherit from `SqlWithChangeLogs` unless it is intentionally read-only reference data (`SqlModel`), intentionally Mongo-native, or an explicitly reviewed exception (e.g. `ApiKey`).
rules.adjustmentNotes: Texto original falava em 'new or materially changed mutable Postgres models', amplo demais e podia comentar em qualquer edicao de model. Restrito a criacao de model novo, mudanca de heranca/base class, ou nova entidade mutavel sem trilha de log; excecoes explicitadas.

rules.title: New Credentials and Sensitive Endpoints Must Follow the Repo's Config Flow
rules.description: A new credential, sensitive endpoint, or operational integration flag should enter through `app/config.py`, have coherent local dev wiring via `.env.dev` or compose, and, when applicable in production, be mapped through `aws/secrets.sh` and workflows/task definitions -- not hardcoded in a service, task, or lib.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Regra original era generica de 'nao hardcode segredo', quase identica a de nimda/jobs-site. Ancorada no fluxo real de config deste repo especifico (config.py + .env.dev + aws/secrets.sh) para deixar de ser mais uma versao redundante.

rules.title: Ship Focused Pytest Coverage With Higher-Risk Service And Model Changes
rules.description: Changes to transactional multi-model services, Celery/external-provider flows, prospecting/LLM logic, or data-shaping/sanitization helpers should add or update focused pytest coverage using the repo's real testing style (`MockPG`, `ModelMock`, fake gateways, deterministic provider mocks). When the change clearly falls under backend-api-01 or backend-api-02, those rules should be the primary comment, not this one.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Reduzida de cobranca ampla para escopo de maior risco, evitando redundancia com as regras de cobertura ja calibradas em nimda/jobs-manager.

rules.title: Use TanStack Query for Async Client Server State
rules.description: Client-side async server state was implemented with ad hoc useEffect/fetch or stored in arbitrary React state/context instead of using TanStack Query queries, mutations, and cache invalidation patterns already established in the repo.

rules.title: Keep the Contractor Realtime Router Transport-Only
rules.description: Code in the contractor realtime layer started storing domain entities or performing feature-specific cache invalidation directly instead of emitting normalized events and delegating reactions to feature code.

rules.title: Keep Legacy Manager and Jobs-Chat Auth on the Server
rules.description: A change moved authenticated legacy integration calls or credential resolution into client code, exposed server-only env vars or tokens, or stopped forwarding the server-side session context that the legacy backends expect.

rules.title: Treat NEXT_PUBLIC Vars and Standalone Output as a Build-Time Contract
rules.description: A config or runtime change assumed NEXT_PUBLIC variables can vary without rebuild, or added runtime files and dependencies without considering standalone tracing and Docker copy behavior.

rules.title: Public UX in Portuguese, Technical Code in English
rules.description: New user-facing text/routes in `app/**`, `components/**`, or `features/**` should be in Brazilian Portuguese, and new code identifiers (components, hooks, helpers, types) should be in English. Do not flag test descriptions, already-stabilized legacy names, or fields inherited from legacy backend payloads.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Restrito a texto visivel ao usuario e nomes de codigo novos; explicitamente exclui nitpick em descricao de teste, nomes historicos e campos herdados de payload legado.

rules.title: Hook-Orchestrated State, View-Only Components
rules.description: A change moved domain orchestration, async flow, or composition of multiple hooks directly into a view component instead of a dedicated orchestrator hook. Strictly local UI state (e.g. a toggle, an open/closed flag) remains acceptable in components. Main hotspots: demands shell, demand-chat shell, contractor header/notifications, and components that already depend on orchestrator hooks.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Original podia comentar contra qualquer useState/useEffect local; ajustado para so disparar quando orquestracao de dominio/async migra para o componente de view. Deixa de sobrepor 'Keep App Router Files Thin' (que foi descartada).

rules.title: Place Components by Scope: primitives, common, then feature
rules.description: Comment mainly on extraction/reuse decisions: UI that is clearly feature-local being promoted too early to `components/common`, business-specific UI landing in `components/primitives`, or a new shared component being born already coupled to a single feature's payload or flow.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Reformulada para focar em decisao de extracao/reuso, nao em estetica de estrutura de pasta.

rules.title: Keep Unit-Testable Boundaries Covered with Vitest
rules.description: Changes to adapters/parsers, pure helpers, cache patch/merge logic, realtime normalization/routing, or reusable primitives with non-trivial behavior should add or update colocated Vitest coverage. Do not use this rule to demand coverage for any `.tsx` change, thin route/layout composition, or auth-dependent async flows that don't yet have an E2E lane.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Original cobria qualquer arquivo ts/tsx, repetindo a cobranca generica de teste ja estreitada em outros repos. Restrito a boundaries deterministicos e testaveis do padrao real do repo.

rules.title: Promo And PIX Subscription Semantics Must Stay Explicit
rules.description: A change touches promotional or PIX subscription logic without preserving or clearly documenting the current semantics for `vigencia_promo`, paid-payment counting, local migration without `iugu_assinatura_id`, and PIX/manual payment filtering.

rules.title: Webhook Auth Must Preserve Integrity Checks And Key Hygiene
rules.description: A change to webhook authorization or decryption bypasses MAC verification, weakens key normalization or length checks, skips invalid-payload handling, or hard-codes secrets instead of reading them from configuration.

rules.title: PHP Changes Must Remain Compatible With The Docker Runtime
rules.description: A PHP change relies on PHP 8+ syntax, extensions, or behavior without an accompanying runtime and tooling upgrade for the 7.4-based Docker and test environment.

rules.title: Subscription And Payment Flows Must Stay Owned By Assinatura/Pagamento
rules.description: A change reimplements or unnecessarily bypasses an existing semantics already owned by the `Assinatura`/`Pagamento` models (promo migration, PIX renewal, payment filters, Iugu plan sync/migration), or grows that logic in a shell/controller when a domain method already owns the flow.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Original era regra ampla de 'business logic outside model', quase igual a de jobs-site. Estreitada para os fluxos ja centralizados em Assinatura/Pagamento neste repo, deixando de ser mais uma versao da familia generica.

rules.title: Transition Side Effects Should Use Events Or Shared Libs
rules.description: A change bypasses an existing `dispararEvento()` dispatch or a listener registered in `Config/events.php`, alters a payload consumed by a listener without aligning the other side, or duplicates a side effect instead of reusing `EmailLib` or the listener that already owns the flow.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Restrito a transicoes ja explicitamente event-driven ou com seam compartilhado claro, para nao competir com a regra equivalente de nimda nem com a de evento/listener de jobs-manager.

rules.title: High-Risk Domain Changes Need Focused PHPUnit Coverage
rules.description: A change to `Assinatura`, `WebhookAuthorizationLib`, payment/notification/webhook listeners, or PIX/promo flows and workers that alter operational behavior shipped without focused PHPUnit coverage in the repo's real test style (`AssinaturaTest`, `WebhookTest`, `WebhookAuthorizationLibTest`, or shell/model tests when there is an explicit operational contract).
rules.adjustmentNotes: Reduzida de high generico para medium e restrita as areas de maior risco, para nao repetir a mesma cobranca generica de teste ja presente em outros repos validados.

rules.title: Mautic Payload Must Preserve Explicit Null And Placeholder Fields
rules.description: A change to Mautic payload-building logic filters the payload with `array_filter()`, drops explicit `null` or `'-'` values, or changes merge order so field-clearing semantics stop reaching Mautic. In this repo those falsy values are intentional in flows like `cadastrarAtualizarUsuarioMautic()` and are covered by focused tests.
rules.adjustmentNotes: A regra default antiga era uma proibicao generica de `array_filter`; aqui ela foi reescrita para a semantica real do payload Mautic deste repo.

rules.title: CakePHP 2 File/Class Naming Mismatch
rules.description: In CakePHP 2, the filename must exactly match the class name including case. A mismatch causes silent load failures. Bad: file `emailSender.php` containing `class EmailSender`. Good: file `EmailSender.php` containing `class EmailSender`.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Movida da baseline default para repo-specific porque esta cobranca so faz sentido nos repos legados em CakePHP 2.

rules.title: CakePHP 2 Undeclared $uses or $components
rules.description: A Model or Component is used inside a CakePHP 2 controller without being declared in $uses or $components. This causes a fatal error at runtime. Bad: calling `$this->Payment->save()` without `public $uses = ["Payment"]`. Good: declare every Model in `$uses` and every Component in `$components` before using them.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Movida da baseline default para repo-specific porque esta cobranca so faz sentido nos repos legados em CakePHP 2.

rules.title: Business Logic Outside Model Layer
rules.description: A domain calculation, eligibility check, or status transition was added in a controller or view instead of a model or model-backed service boundary.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Removida a mencao a 'persistence workflow' para nao sobrepor jobs-site-07, que ja e a regra estreita de find/save/updateAll/deleteAll encadeado em controller/component. jobs-site-01 fica com calculo de dominio, eligibility check e status transition; jobs-site-07 fica com o workflow de persistencia/lookup.

rules.title: Unscoped CakePHP Query Or Overfetch
rules.description: A new or changed find or paginate query omits intentional fields, contain, recursive, order, or limits where the call site needs them, increasing overfetching or N+1 risk.

rules.title: Manual SQL Or Unsafe Query Construction
rules.description: A new Model::query path or string-built SQL statement is introduced without a strong reason, array-based conditions, and safe escaping of all dynamic values.

rules.title: Magic Status Or Payment IDs Instead Of Named Constants
rules.description: A diff introduces or expands a status, payment-form, or transfer ID branch without reusing the named constant already defined on the matching model (Assinatura/Pagamento and related), even though an equivalent constant exists nearby.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Restrita a reuso de constante ja existente no model, para nao sobrepor a nova regra default 'Magic Number Without Named Constant' (que cobre o caso generico de extrair um literal para constante quando nenhuma existe ainda).

rules.title: Auth Session Or Route Change Without Global Boundary Review
rules.description: A login, permission, session, maintenance, canonical-host, or redirect change is made without reviewing AppController, routes.php, and the server-side authorization checks that guard the affected flow.

rules.title: Config Change Without Env Wiring Or Secret Hygiene
rules.description: A new configuration value is added without env-based wiring, without updating php/.env.dev, aws/secrets.sh, and docs/ENV_REFERENCE.md by name only, or by committing a real secret or hardcoded credential.

rules.title: Controller Adds Ad Hoc Persistence Or Lookup Workflow
rules.description: A controller or component chains new find/save/updateAll/deleteAll read-write workflow logic that should instead become a reusable model method testable in isolation.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Encosta bastante em jobs-site-01 (business logic outside model) e na regra de query/performance; mantida separada por foco (workflow encadeado especifico), mas pode ser fundida no futuro se gerar comentario duplicado no mesmo diff.

rules.title: High-Risk Behavior Change Without Fixture Or Mock Coverage
rules.description: A DB-backed behavior change in auth, billing, model callbacks, external integrations, or listeners/shared libs lands without the necessary fixtures for queried models, or an external integration path is tested with production-only hooks instead of mocking the boundary.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Reduzida de high generico para medium e restrita a auth, billing, callbacks de model, integracoes externas e listeners/libs compartilhadas -- ficou ampla demais como veio para um repo legado deste tamanho.

rules.title: User Controlled Data Must Be Validated On Input And Escaped On Output
rules.description: User-controlled data reaches persistence or rendering without server-side validation/normalization and context-appropriate escaping. Trigger mainly at trust-boundary crossings: persistence of user input, rendering of user-controlled content, and HTML/URL/email/payloads flowing out to templates. Keep `high` only when there is a clear risk of XSS, template injection, sensitive data exposure, or missing validation.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Original podia comentar em excesso em qualquer diff de controller/model/view/email. Restrita a mudancas de trust boundary de verdade.

rules.title: New Jobs Site Migration Or Vendor Patch
rules.description: A diff adds a new app migration (schema work belongs in jobs-database, not here) or edits vendor/Composer-installed dependency code in place instead of fixing the package at its source. For php/app/Plugin/**, only flag when the reviewer has confirmed the plugin is Composer-installed rather than team-owned local code -- do not block any app/Plugin change automatically.
rules.adjustmentNotes: fileGlobs original incluia php/app/Plugin/**/* como bloqueio bruto, mas nesse repo app/Plugin mistura pacote instalado com plugin local. Removido do bloqueio automatico; mantido so como verificacao manual do reviewer.

rules.title: CakePHP 2 File/Class Naming Mismatch
rules.description: In CakePHP 2, the filename must exactly match the class name including case. A mismatch causes silent load failures. Bad: file `emailSender.php` containing `class EmailSender`. Good: file `EmailSender.php` containing `class EmailSender`.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Movida da baseline default para repo-specific porque esta cobranca so faz sentido nos repos legados em CakePHP 2.

rules.title: CakePHP 2 Undeclared $uses or $components
rules.description: A Model or Component is used inside a CakePHP 2 controller without being declared in $uses or $components. This causes a fatal error at runtime. Bad: calling `$this->Payment->save()` without `public $uses = ["Payment"]`. Good: declare every Model in `$uses` and every Component in `$components` before using them.
rules.adjustmentNotes: Movida da baseline default para repo-specific porque esta cobranca so faz sentido nos repos legados em CakePHP 2.

discardedRules.note: Listadas apenas para referencia/auditoria -- NAO estao ativas e nao devem ser reimportadas sem nova decisao.
jobs-manager: Keep Controllers Thin and Domain Logic Reusable
jobs-database: Use create() or update() Inside change() Migrations
jobs-chat: Keep Controllers Thin and Delegate Domain Flow
backend-api: Retriable Celery Tasks For External IO
Update Runtime Docs When Local Topology Changes
jc-webapp: Preserve a Single Root proxy.ts Entry
Use Repo Tokens and Breakpoints Instead of Ad Hoc Tailwind Values
jobs-robots: Shell Actions Should Orchestrate And Reuse Tasks
Cake Queries Must Declare Their Fetch Scope
